title: Cleome augustinensis
intro: Cleome augustinensis, also known as African Spider Flower, is a species of flowering plant in the Cleome genus, characterized by its unique, delicate flowers and ability to thrive in a variety of environments. Traditionally, Cleome augustinensis has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, thanks to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cleome augustinensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Cleome augustinensis is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and its extracts are also used in the production of capsules, tablets, and other herbal supplements.
taxonomy: Cleome augustinensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cleomaceae. The genus Cleome is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Cleome augustinensis is a species of Cleome that is native to Madagascar, and it is often cultivated for its vibrant flowers and ornamental value. The plant is a member of the order Brassicales and is closely related to other plants in the family Cleomaceae. Cleome augustinensis is also known to be a part of the APG III classification.
